description_long: This is a single, ripe pear with a bulbous lower half tapering to a narrower top where a short, brown stem protrudes. The skin is predominantly yellow-green with a scattering of brown speckles and some subtle russet patches, especially near the top. The surface appears slightly matte with a gentle, natural sheen, and the texture is semi-smooth with visible micro-roughness typical of pear skin. There are a few minor blemishes and natural marks, indicating it is a real, fresh fruit. The overall shape is asymmetrical, with one side slightly more rounded and the other showing a gentle curve inward. The pear looks plump and juicy, suggesting it is in good edible condition.
description: A ripe, yellow-green pear with brown speckles, a short brown stem, and a plump, slightly asymmetrical shape.
description_view_0: The pear is seen from the front, showing its broadest side with a prominent stem and a mix of yellow-green skin and brown speckles.
description_view_1: This view highlights the side with more russet coloration and a slightly more pronounced curve, with the stem angled toward the viewer.
description_view_2: The pear appears narrower from this angle, with visible blemishes and a darker, more golden-brown hue on the upper half.
description_view_3: This view returns to a more symmetrical appearance, with the yellow-green skin and speckles evenly distributed and the stem upright.
attribute_contextual_placement: stacked with other pears in a crate or displayed in a fruit bowl
attribute_condition: fresh and ripe with minor natural blemishes
